raw.inscription: James J Chance, MA, JP, DL. For 50 years, a partner in Chance Brothers & Co at the Glass Works Smethwick, and Akali Works, Oldbury. He purchased the land for the Park, laid it out and endowed it and on Sep 7 1895 opened it as a gift to the public forever.
raw.name: James Chance Memorial
raw.official_name: Memorial to James Chance in West Smethwick Park

n: Flt. Sgt. Allan Charlie Cox & Flt. Sgt. Gordon Preston Memorial
q: Flt. Sgt. Allan Charlie Cox & Flt. Sgt. Gordon Preston Memorial Birmingham

raw.inscription: Not Forgotten/ 60th Anniversary 2004/ This stone is laid in/ remembrance of/ Flt. Sgt. ALLAN CHARLIE COX/ Of Smethwick/ And/ Flt. Sgt. GORFON PRESTON/ Of Pinner (Middlesex)/ who's plane crashed near/ this spot on 31st July 1944
raw.name: Flt. Sgt. Allan Charlie Cox & Flt. Sgt. Gordon Preston Memorial
raw.note: Commemoration to Ft Sgts Cox and Preston who crashed near this spot 31 July 1944

raw.inscription:
The people of Sandwell
Commemorate those who
Died in the Holocaust,
As a result of Nazi
Persecution and in other
Genocides.

There are some who have
no memorial.
They are perished as though
they had never been.

Forget them not

raw.description: The pylon rises from a five-stepped base, rectangular on plan, and at the top dies back to the foot of the empty tomb. The tomb is in the form of a tapered Classical chest, ornamented with hemispherical bosses.
raw.inscription:
East Face: IN MEMORY OF/ THE MEN OF/ OLDBURY/ WHO FELL IN THE/ GREAT WARS/ 1914-1918/ AND/ 1939-1945/ AND IN/ ALL OTHER/ CONFLICTS. 
West Face: AT THE/ GOING DOWN/ OF THE SUN/ AND IN THE/ MORNING WE WILL/ REMEMBER THEM.
raw.name: Oldbury Cenotaph
